label.purpose: Eyebrows, metadata, nav labels, and compact UI text.

motion.name: state-quick
motion.purpose: Button hover and header color transitions.

breakpoints.name: small-phone
breakpoints.purpose: Tightens type, header, actions, and contact text for the smallest expected phones.

breakpoints.name: phone
breakpoints.purpose: Collapses the site to one clear column with scroll-safe navigation and full-width action groups.

breakpoints.name: tablet
breakpoints.purpose: Uses six-track editorial grids and two-column masonry instead of cramped desktop compositions.

breakpoints.name: wide
breakpoints.purpose: Expands the canvas slightly while keeping photography bounded and intentional.

breakpoints.name: short-landscape
breakpoints.purpose: Protects hero and page-hero spacing on phones and tablets held sideways.

components.name: Dark Button
components.description: Primary practical action such as viewing the portfolio.
components.css: .ds-button { display: inline-flex; min-height: 46px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 0.8rem 1.05rem; border: 1px solid #171715; border-radius: 0; font-family: Onest, Segoe UI, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; transition: transform 180ms ease-out, background-color 180ms ease-out, color 180ms ease-out; } .ds-button:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #c99d37; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-button-dark { background: #171715; color: #fdf9ef; }

components.name: Gold Button
components.description: Buying or sales-adjacent action. Use sparingly.
components.css: .ds-button { display: inline-flex; min-height: 46px; align-items: center; justify-content: center; padding: 0.8rem 1.05rem; border: 1px solid #c99d37; border-radius: 0; font-family: Onest, Segoe UI, sans-serif; font-weight: 700; transition: transform 180ms ease-out, background-color 180ms ease-out, color 180ms ease-out; } .ds-button:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-button:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #171715; outline-offset: 3px; } .ds-button-gold { background: #c99d37; color: #171715; }

components.name: Header Navigation
components.description: Fixed wordmark and practical navigation for portfolio, buy photos, and contact, with aria-current on the active route.
components.html: Jamie Kazemier PhotographyPortfolioBuy PhotosContact
components.css: .ds-nav { display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between; gap: 1rem; padding: 1rem 2rem; color: #171715; background: rgba(248, 242, 231, 0.96); border-bottom: 1px solid #d7c9b1; font-family: Onest, Segoe UI, sans-serif; } .ds-wordmark { font-family: Schibsted Grotesk, Arial Narrow, sans-serif; font-size: 1.35rem; font-weight: 800; color: inherit; text-decoration: none; } .ds-links { display: flex; gap: 1.25rem; font-size: 0.86rem; } .ds-links a { color: inherit; min-height: 44px; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; text-decoration: none; } .ds-links a:hover { color: #745925; }

components.name: Gallery Card
components.description: An image-led event gallery entry with metadata and a large modern grotesk title.
components.css: .ds-gallery-card { display: grid; gap: 0.65rem; padding-top: 0.85rem; border-top: 1px solid #d7c9b1; font-family: Onest, Segoe UI, sans-serif; color: #171715; } .ds-gallery-image { aspect-ratio: 4 / 5; background: #c2cfcb; } .ds-gallery-card span { color: #736b61; font-size: 0.85rem; } .ds-gallery-card strong { font-family: Schibsted Grotesk, Arial Narrow, sans-serif; font-size: 2rem; font-weight: 800; line-height: 0.92; }

components.name: Contact Tile
components.description: Large text-led contact route for email and Instagram.
components.css: .ds-contact-tile { display: grid; gap: 0.5rem; padding: 1rem 0 2rem; border-top: 1px solid #d7c9b1; color: #171715; text-decoration: none; } .ds-contact-tile span { color: #736b61; font-family: Onest, Segoe UI, sans-serif; font-size: 0.85rem; } .ds-contact-tile strong { font-family: Schibsted Grotesk, Arial Narrow, sans-serif; font-size: clamp(2rem, 5vw, 3.8rem); font-weight: 800; line-height: 0.92; overflow-wrap: anywhere; }

narrative.overview:
The system should feel like an edited wall of rowing photographs in a quiet studio: warm paper, deep ink, measured spacing, gold used as a deliberate mark, and images treated as authored objects. It is a brand surface first and a gallery index second. The design must make race photos easy to find without flattening the work into a generic event-upload grid.

The current implementation is art-directed but calmer than the first bold pass. Future polish should tune crop choices, spacing, and mobile rhythm rather than retreating to safe symmetry. PRODUCT.md says the site must not feel "generic, bland, template-like"; that line is binding.
keyCharacteristics: Image-led, with hero and gallery photography carrying the first impression.
Warm, premium neutrals with a muted rowing-club palette and precise gold accent.
Flat, editorial surfaces with rules and image scale instead of decorative shadows.
Practical event browsing that still feels curated and made with intent.
Layout rhythm built from shared gutter, content, wide, and section spacing tokens rather than one-off padding.
Responsive behavior adapts the composition for phone, tablet, desktop, wide, and short-landscape contexts.
First impression pairs a pure hero photograph with a quiet rowing-context strip rather than overlay text.
Photography is served with responsive AVIF, WebP, and JPG srcsets.

rules.name: The Gold Has To Earn It Rule
rules.body: Gold is for buying, wayfinding, and authored emphasis. Do not sprinkle it as decoration.

rules.name: The No Sterile White Rule
rules.body: Never introduce pure white or pure black. Neutrals stay warm and photographic.

rules.name: The Authored Caption Rule
rules.body: Text should sound selected, not generated. Short, specific, visual lines beat explanatory paragraphs.

rules.name: The Modern Grotesk Rule
rules.body: The brand voice is premium-modern, not ornamental. Use Schibsted Grotesk for display scale and Onest for calm supporting text.

rules.name: The Quiet Monument Rule
rules.body: Only one element per view should feel monumental. Reduce weight, rule thickness, and saturation before removing character.

rules.name: The External Shop Rule
rules.body: Buy Photos should route to contact for now and later to Pixieset or Oypo. Do not imply onsite checkout.

rules.name: The Named Derivative Rule
rules.body: Transparent overlays, dark panels, quiet rules, and softened text colors should live as named CSS variables instead of scattered one-off values.

rules.name: The No Floating Gallery Rule
rules.body: Do not add card shadows to portfolio or gallery items. A photography portfolio should feel printed and sequenced, not like floating SaaS cards.

dos: Do lead with real rowing imagery and treat every image as an authored object.
Do use Warm Photo Paper, Dark Hull Ink, softened club colors, and Regatta Gold as the recognizable core.
Do quiet bold moments by thinning rules, softening saturation, and reducing weight before making the layout generic.
Do use rowing-specific interface language: blade timing, crew movement, race-day coverage, finish-line pressure, event names, and race details.
Do keep event browsing practical for rowers and families looking for photos.
Do use asymmetry, stronger crops, and varied pacing on desktop, then simplify to one clear column on mobile.
Do keep every interactive target at least 44px tall and make phone actions full-width when space is tight.
Do preserve alt text, contrast, keyboard navigation, and reduced-motion support.
donts: Don't make the site feel "generic, bland, template-like, or like a default photography grid with swapped-in images."
Don't add decorative card shadows, glass effects, gradient text, or side-stripe borders.
Don't let buying feel like a dead placeholder. It must always route to contact or a real sales destination.
Don't imply checkout happens on the portfolio site while sales are planned for Pixieset or Oypo.
Don't retreat to polite centered sections, equal cards, safe serif typography, or ornamental fashion type if the site starts feeling bland again.
